heading: The Room Wants a
Name
warmup.intro: Last class you escalated, and you closed like this:

callback.text: I am telling her before anybody calls anybody.
callback.speak: I am telling her before anybody calls anybody.

warmup.box_title: Today you are the one in the chair, and the room wants somebody to blame.
warmup.box_text: Steering committee. The go-live moved twice. Three directors, twenty minutes, and the first question is: "Whose fault is this?"

goal.d: Eight words for the hot seat
Three role-plays, and they look for blood

heading: The Language of the Hot
Seat
vocab_ch.sub: 8 words to answer hard questions and keep your credibility

vocab.def: The person who answers for the result, good or bad
vocab.def_pc: the person who answers for the result, good or bad
vocab.hint: The person who answers for the result
vocab.ex: I am accountable for this date.

vocab.def: What people say when they think you did badly
vocab.def_pc: what people say when they think you did badly
vocab.hint: What people say when you did badly
vocab.ex: Criticism is information. Listen, and then answer.

vocab.def: To explain and protect a decision, with facts
vocab.def_pc: to explain and protect a decision, with facts
vocab.hint: To protect a decision with facts
vocab.ex: I can defend every date in this plan.

vocab.def: To say that a person is the cause of the problem
vocab.def_pc: to say that a person is the cause of the problem
vocab.hint: To say a person caused the problem
vocab.ex: The room wants to blame somebody. Do not join.

vocab.def: Yes, no, or a number -- before the explanation
vocab.def_pc: yes, no, or a number: before the explanation
vocab.hint: Yes, no, or a number -- first
vocab.ex: Give them a straight answer, then the reason.

vocab.def: Steady: your voice does not change under attack
vocab.def_pc: steady: your voice does not change under attack
vocab.hint: Your voice does not change under attack
vocab.ex: Stay calm. The calm person owns the room.

vocab.def: To keep supporting something, even under pressure
vocab.def_pc: to keep supporting something, even under pressure
vocab.hint: To keep supporting it under pressure
vocab.ex: I stand by the decision, and here is why.

vocab.def: The trust people give you, built answer by answer
vocab.def_pc: the trust people give you, built answer by answer
vocab.ex: One honest "I do not know" saves your credibility.

heading: The Meeting You Left
Bleeding
vocab_check.prompt: Think of a meeting at Oracle where you were attacked in front of other people.
vocab_check.sub: Talk for 90 seconds: what was the question, what did you answer, and what do you wish you had said?

reading.ch_sub: A short text about the twenty minutes that decide your reputation
heading: The Room Is Not the
Enemy
reading.rtitle: The Room Is Not the Enemy
paras: In a steering committee, the questions sound like attacks, and the first instinct is to defend yourself. That instinct is the enemy. The moment you start explaining before you answer, the room decides you are hiding something.

So answer first. Yes, no, or a number -- one sentence, and then the reason. If you do not know, say so, and give the hour you will send it: 'I do not have that number now. You will have it by five.' An honest 'I do not know' costs you nothing. A guess that turns out wrong costs you everything.

And when the mistake is yours, own it in four words -- 'that is on me' -- then move immediately to what you are doing about it. Nobody in that room is looking for a perfect project. They are looking for somebody who is accountable, calm, and impossible to surprise.

choices: Explain the context first, so the committee understands the difficulty.
Answer first -- yes, no, or a number -- own your mistakes, and never guess.
Never admit a mistake in front of directors.

tf: You should explain the background before you answer the question.
Answer first: yes, no, or a number. Then the reason.
"I do not know -- you will have it by five" is a strong answer.
An honest "I do not know" costs nothing. A wrong guess costs everything.
When the mistake is yours, you should own it and move to the plan.
'That is on me' -- four words, then what you are doing about it.

heading: How to Survive the Hot
Seat
bank.sub: The straight answer → the reason → what you own → the number or the hour → the plan
bank.label: The five lines of a defence
items: Yes. The go-live moved twice, and I am accountable for the date.
The reason is the database approval -- eleven days of silence, and four weeks last year.
The first delay is on me: I should have escalated a week earlier.
I do not have that number now. You will have it by five o'clock.
I stand by June 27th, and here is what protects it.
If you want a different date, I need one of two things -- and I can explain both.

grammar.prompt: Which one answers? Which one owns it? Which one buys you the time you need?

discovery.html: "Yes. The go-live moved twice, and I am accountable for the date."
discovery.speak: Yes. The go-live moved twice, and I am accountable for the date.

discovery.html: "That is on me. I should have escalated a week earlier."
discovery.speak: That is on me. I should have escalated a week earlier.

discovery.html: "I do not have that number now. You will have it by five."
discovery.speak: I do not have that number now. You will have it by five.

discovery.html: "Let me explain what protects the new date."
discovery.speak: Let me explain what protects the new date.

rows: Yes / No / a number -- one sentence, then the reason
Yes. The go-live moved twice. The reason is the approval.
Own the part that is yours
That is on me + I should have + past participle
That is on me. I should have escalated earlier.
I do not have that number now. + I will send it by + hour
I do not have it now. I will send it by five.
grammar.note: The calm person owns the room. They are not looking for a perfect project -- they are looking for somebody who is accountable, who answers straight, and who never guesses. One honest "I do not know, by five o'clock" is worth more than ten confident guesses.
mistakes: I will send you it tomorrow.
I will send it to you tomorrow.
Let me to explain the number.
grammar.mistake_note: Com send, o pronome objeto vem primeiro: send it to you (nunca "send you it"). E depois de let me vem o verbo puro: let me explain, nunca "let me to explain".

heading: The Hot-Seat
Card
artefact.title: The Three Questions They Will Ask
artefact.reason: Q1 -- "Whose fault is the delay?": the approval, and the first week is on me
rows: Q2 -- "Will June 27th hold?": yes, and here are the two things that protect it
Q3 -- "What does it cost?": I do not have the final number -- you will have it by 5 PM
What I own: I should have escalated one week earlier
What I need: the client's security team on a call this week
artefact.footer: Answer, own, prove. Never guess a number in the room -- and never leave without asking for what you need.

heading: They Are Looking for a
Name
listenings.text: Diogo, twenty minutes, three directors, and I want you to hear this before you walk in. They are not looking for a perfect project. They are looking for a name, so that somebody in this building is responsible. If you defend yourself, they will smell it, and it will take an hour. So do the opposite. Answer the question in one sentence, before you explain anything. Own the part that is yours, in four words, and then move to the plan. If they ask for a number you do not have, do not invent it -- give them an hour, and send it. And whatever happens, keep your voice steady. The calm person owns that room, and after twenty minutes they will not remember your slides. They will remember whether they can trust you.
comp: What is the committee looking for?
A name -- somebody who is responsible.
What should Diogo do if they ask for a number he does not have?
Not invent it: give them an hour, and send it.
What will they remember after twenty minutes?
Not the slides -- whether they can trust him.

heading: The Defence,
Done Well
listenings.lead: This is the target. Listen, then do it your way.
listenings.text: Yes. The go-live moved twice, and I am accountable for the date. The reason is the database approval: the client's security team went silent for eleven days, and the same approval took four weeks last year. Now, the part that is on me: I should have escalated a week earlier. I did not, and that first week is mine. Here is where we are. I stand by June 27th, and two things protect it: the tests are already running on the old database, and the scope is now the standard reports only. You asked what the delay costs. I do not have the final number in front of me -- I will not guess it in this room. You will have it by five o'clock today. And there is one thing I need from this committee: the client's security team on a call this week. Without that call, I cannot protect June 27th, and I would rather tell you now than in September.
comp: What does he say in his first sentence?
Yes -- the go-live moved twice, and he is accountable for the date.
The first week: he should have escalated earlier.
What does he ask the committee for?
The client's security team on a call this week.

heading: Whose Fault Is
This?

lines.html: The go-live moved twice, Diogo. Whose fault is that?
lines.speak: The go-live moved twice, Diogo. Whose fault is that?

lines.html: The approval is the cause. And the first week is on me -- I should have escalated earlier. I am accountable for the date.
lines.speak: The approval is the cause. And the first week is on me. I should have escalated earlier. I am accountable for the date.

lines.html: So you admit the project is out of control.
lines.speak: So you admit the project is out of control.

lines.html: No. I admit one week is mine. I stand by June 27th, and two things protect it.
lines.speak: No. I admit one week is mine. I stand by June 27th, and two things protect it.

lines.html: What does the delay cost us? Give me the number.
lines.speak: What does the delay cost us? Give me the number.

lines.html: I do not have the final number here, and I will not guess it in this room. You will have it by five o'clock.
lines.speak: I do not have the final number here, and I will not guess it in this room. You will have it by five o'clock.

lines.html: The client thinks your team is slow. What do I tell them?
lines.speak: The client thinks your team is slow. What do I tell them?

lines.html: Tell them the tests are already running, and the scope is fixed. And I need one thing from this room: their security team on a call this week.
lines.speak: Tell them the tests are already running, and the scope is fixed. And I need one thing from this room: their security team on a call this week.

lines.html: Fine. You get the call. And Diogo -- that was a straight answer. Keep it that way.
lines.speak: Fine. You get the call. And Diogo, that was a straight answer. Keep it that way.

comp: What is the committee's first question?
Whose fault is it that the go-live moved twice.
What does Diogo refuse to do?
Guess a number in the room.
What does he get from the committee?
The client's security team on a call this week.

quickfire.situation: "Whose fault is this?" Answer in one sentence.
tips: Own the part that is yours.

quickfire.situation: Own your mistake in four words -- then move.
tips: Then: what you are doing about it.

quickfire.situation: They ask for a number you do not have.
tips: 'I will not guess it in this room.'
Give an hour, and keep it.

quickfire.situation: "Is your team slow?" Answer without attacking anyone.
Defend the new date in one sentence.
tips: 'I stand by June 27th, and two things protect it.'

quickfire.situation: Ask the room for the one thing you need.
tips: Never leave the hot seat empty-handed.
Say what you cannot protect without it.

items: Should we replace you as project manager?
You said June 20th, then June 27th. Why should we believe this date?
The client says your team never answers. Is that true?
pressure.followup: Answer first, own second, prove third. Steady voice. Never guess a number, and never blame a person who is not in the room.

heading: Twenty Minutes in the
Chair
guided.prompt: Steering committee. Three directors. Defend the project.
guided.sub: Use the hot-seat card. Five moves, in this order:
chips: Yes -- I am accountable for the date
I will send it by five
I need the security team on a call

heading: They Smell
Blood
semi.quote: So you admit the project is out of control. Should we replace you?
semi.sub: They attack twice: first they twist your honesty into an admission of failure, then they ask for a number you do not have. Stay calm, answer first, refuse to guess, and leave with the one thing you need from the room.
chips: No. One week is mine, not the project
I will not guess in this room
you will have it by five
what I need from you is...

heading: Your Real
Committee
free.headline: Two minutes. No notes. No excuses.
free.sub: Take a real problem on your project at Oracle -- a delay, an overspend, a bad decision. Defend it out loud in front of directors: the straight answer, the reason, what you own, what you will not guess, and the one thing you need from them.

survival.en: Yes. I am accountable for the date.
survival.pt: Sim. Eu sou o responsável pela data.

survival.en: That is on me. I should have escalated earlier.
survival.pt: Isso é responsabilidade minha. Eu deveria ter escalado antes.

survival.en: I do not have that number now. You will have it by five.
survival.pt: Não tenho esse número agora. Você terá até as cinco.

survival.en: I stand by June 27th, and two things protect it.
survival.pt: Eu sustento 27 de junho, e duas coisas protegem essa data.

survival.en: I need one thing from this room.
survival.pt: Preciso de uma coisa desta sala.

checks: I answer first -- yes, no, or a number -- and explain after.
I own my part in four words: "That is on me."
I never guess a number: I give an hour, and I send it.
I keep my voice steady when the questions are hostile.
I never leave the room without asking for what I need.

closing.line: Twenty lessons, twenty conversations you used to avoid. Today they attacked, and you answered straight.
closing.next_desc: Twenty done. From here: leading the room, not just surviving it.

order_audio.text: The committee asks whose fault the delay is. Diogo answers straight, and says the first week is on him. He stands by June 27th, and names the two things that protect it. When they ask for a number he does not have, he refuses to guess and promises it by five o'clock. Finally, he asks the room for the client's security team on a call this week.
items: Diogo answers straight, and says the first week is on him.
He refuses to guess the number, and promises it by five o'clock.
The committee asks whose fault the delay is.
He asks the room for the security team on a call this week.
He stands by June 27th, and names the two things that protect it.

preclass.desc: Survive the steering committee: answer straight, own your part, never guess, and ask for what you need.
preclass.context: Three directors, twenty minutes, and the first question is an attack: whose fault is the delay? The instinct is to defend yourself and explain the context. That instinct loses the room. Diogo gives a straight answer instead: "Yes. The go-live moved twice, and I am accountable for the date." Then the reason -- the approval -- and then the part that is his: "That is on me. I should have escalated a week earlier." He does not blame anybody who is not in the room. He stands by June 27th, and names the two things that protect it. When they ask for a number he does not have, he refuses to guess: "You will have it by five o'clock." That honesty is what builds his credibility. He stays calm -- the calm person owns the room -- and he takes the criticism as information. And he does not leave without asking for the one thing he needs: the client's security team, on a call, this week.
quiz_ctx: Why does Diogo answer "Yes" before explaining?
Because explaining before answering makes the room think you are hiding something.
Because the delay was not important.
Because he wants the meeting to end quickly.
I will send it to you by five.
I will send you it by five.
I will you send it by five.
They ask for a number he does not have. He:
Says he will not guess, and gives an hour to send it.
Gives an approximate number, to look confident.
Says the finance team should answer that.
preclass.tip_sub: Sobreviver ao comitê: responder direto, assumir sua parte e nunca chutar número. / Surviving the hot seat.
tip_rows: Yes / No / um número -- uma frase, e SÓ ENTÃO o motivo
That is on me + I should have + particípio
That is on me. I should have escalated earlier.
I do not have that number now. + I will send it by + hora
preclass.tip_key: Com send: send it TO you (nunca "send you it"). Depois de let me, verbo puro: let me explain. E a regra que salva a reputação: um "I do not know, by five o'clock" honesto vale mais do que dez chutes confiantes.

blanks.hint: Hint: the person who answers for the result / responsável, presta contas
blanks.phrase: Yes. I am accountable for the date.

blanks.hint: Hint: four words to own a mistake: that is ___ / é minha responsabilidade
blanks.phrase: That is on me. I should have escalated earlier.

blanks.pre: "I do not have that number now. I will send it
blanks.hint: Hint: send it ___ by five (never "send you it")
blanks.phrase: I will send it to you by five.

blanks.hint: Hint: to keep supporting it under pressure / sustentar
blanks.phrase: I stand by June 27th, and two things protect it.
blanks.post: June 27th, and two things protect it."

blanks.phrase: Let me explain what protects the new date.

blanks.hint: Hint: steady, voice does not change / calmo
blanks.phrase: Stay calm. The calm person owns the room.
blanks.post: . The calm person owns the room."

preclass.speech_intro: The five lines of the hot seat. Record each one. Steady, slow, no apology in the voice -- the calm person owns the room.
quiz_sit: A director asks: "Whose fault is the delay?" You:
Answer straight, name the cause, and own the part that is yours.
Explain the context for three minutes, then answer.
Say it was the vendor, the client and the security team.
They ask for a number you do not have. You:
"I will not guess it in this room. You will have it by five."
Give an approximate number, to look confident.
Say nothing, and hope they forget.
Before you leave the hot seat, you should:
Ask for the one thing you need to protect the date.
Promise a date you are not sure about.
think: Take a real problem on your project at Oracle -- a delay, an overspend, a decision that failed. Defend it out loud, for two minutes, in front of three directors: (1) the straight answer, (2) the reason, (3) the part you own, (4) the number you will NOT guess -- and when you will send it, (5) the one thing you need from them. Record it.

Now a director says: "So you admit the project is out of control. Should we replace you?" Answer out loud. Do not defend yourself, do not raise your voice -- answer straight, hold the date, and say what protects it. Record it.

complementary.title: Steve Jobs (2015)
complementary.desc: Jobs is attacked by the board and by colleagues before a big launch. Watch how he answers hard questions and defends his plan.
complementary.tip: Pick one confrontation scene and note how he stays calm and returns to his main point.

complementary.title: Skills 360 -- Defending Your Ideas (Part 1)
complementary.desc: How to protect a good idea when other people criticize it or compete with you.
complementary.tip: Listen and write two sentences to defend your project with evidence, not emotion.

complementary.title: How to Handle Tough Questions in a Presentation
complementary.desc: Practical ways to stay calm and confident when the audience puts you on the spot.
complementary.tip: Prepare answers for the 3 hardest questions your steering committee could ask you.

teacher.title: Abertura (2 min): Compartilhe a tela. Diga: 'Twenty lessons. Today is the exam: three directors, twenty minutes, and they want a name.' Avise: hoje ele fala quase o tempo todo -- e voce vai atacar.
teacher.warmup: Warm-up falado (4 min): Toque a frase da Aula 19. Depois a virada: 'Now YOU are the one in the chair.' Faca a pergunta do box e cale a boca 90 segundos.
teacher.goal: Objetivo (1 min): 'Words, a short text, and then twenty minutes in the hot seat -- three times.' Rapido.
teacher.vocab_transition: Transicao vocab (1 min): 'Eight words. Click each card.' Ferramenta, nao aula de vocabulario.
teacher.vocab1: Vocab reveal 1-4 (4 min): Pista, tentativa, reveal, audio, uma frase real. CCQ 'accountable': 'Is accountable the same as guilty? (No -- it is the person who ANSWERS for the result.)' CCQ 'blame': 'Does blaming someone who is not in the room help you? (Never.)'
teacher.vocab2: Vocab reveal 5-8 (4 min): Mesma dinamica. CCQ 'straight answer': 'Does it come before or after the explanation? (Before.)' CCQ 'credibility': 'Does one honest "I do not know" destroy credibility? (No -- a wrong guess does.)'
teacher.vocab_check: Vocab check falado (4 min): 90 segundos sobre uma reuniao em que ele foi atacado na frente de outras pessoas. Minimo cinco palavras novas. Voce so escuta e conta.
teacher.ch3: Transicao leitura (1 min): 'Short text. Read it fast -- then tell me if you could really say "that is on me" in front of three directors.' A leitura e trampolim.
teacher.reading: Leitura + gist (5 min): 90 segundos de leitura silenciosa. Depois o gist. E entao a pergunta que vale a aula: 'In your company, does owning a mistake make you stronger -- or does it get you replaced?' Dois minutos de fala dele. Discorde de proposito.
teacher.tf: True / False (3 min): Ele decide em voz alta ANTES de clicar, e justifica com uma frase do texto. Rapido.
teacher.bank: Useful language falado (4 min): Cada frase lida em voz alta e imediatamente reescrita para um problema REAL do projeto dele. Seis frases da tela, seis frases dele.
teacher.grammar: Grammar discovery (4 min): Toque as 4 frases. Pergunta unica: 'Which one answers? Which one owns it? Which one buys the time you need?' Guie: Yes/No primeiro; that is on me; I will send it by five.
teacher.rule: Reveal the Rule (3 min): Clique. Responder, assumir, provar. Exija 2 frases dele com 'I should have + participio' sobre erros reais. CCQ: 'Is a wrong guess better than "I do not know"? (No.)'
teacher.mistake: Common Mistake (2 min): 'send you it' e 'let me to explain'. Peca as versoes certas em voz alta, duas vezes.
teacher.ch5: Transicao (1 min): 'Three directors. One delay. Somebody is looking for a name.' Suba a tensao ao maximo.
teacher.listening1: Listening 1 (4 min): 'Sophia tells you what that room really wants.' Toque uma vez, sem texto.
teacher.listening1_comp: Comprehension falada (3 min): Respostas em voz alta antes do clique. Depois: 'What will they remember after twenty minutes?' Deixe ele deduzir falando.
teacher.artefact: Artefato (3 min): Nao leia por ele. 'Read the hot-seat card, then answer the three questions out loud -- one sentence each.' Cronometre: 60 segundos no total.
teacher.dialogue: Dialogo (6 min): Voce e a diretora e voce ATACA: 'whose fault is this?', 'so you admit the project is out of control', 'the client thinks your team is slow'. Antes de cada fala do Diogo, ELE fala primeiro; so entao clique e toque o audio.
teacher.dialogue_comp: Dialogue comprehension (2 min): Perguntas sobre a DIRETORA. Depois: 'Which answer won the room?' Deixe ele defender a resposta.
teacher.listening2: Listening 2 (4 min): 'This is the defence, done well -- 60 seconds under fire.' Toque uma vez. Depois: 'What did he own? What did he refuse to do?' -- ele fala.
teacher.ch6: Transicao (1 min): 'From here they attack, and you answer. Three times, and I will not be kind.'
teacher.quickfire: Quick Fire (5 min): Uma situacao por vez, resposta imediata. Sem correcao entre elas -- fluencia primeiro.
teacher.rp_guided: Role-play GUIADO (4 min): Voce e o comite. Ele defende o projeto com os cinco movimentos do hot-seat card. Se ele explicar ANTES de responder, pare e mande recomecar.
teacher.rp_semi: Role-play SEMI-LIVRE (5 min): Voce ataca duas vezes: transforma a honestidade dele em confissao de fracasso, e depois exige um numero que ele nao tem. Ele nao pode chutar, nem levantar a voz. Tem de sair da sala com o pedido feito.
teacher.rp_free: Role-play LIVRE (5 min): Sem apoio. 'A real problem on your project. Two minutes. No excuses. Go.' NAO interrompa, cronometre e diga o tempo no fim. So entao 2 correcoes.
teacher.pressure: Under pressure (4 min): Tres perguntas hostis, uma a uma, sem aviso ('Should we replace you?'). Se a voz dele mudar, pare e mande repetir com voz firme -- e o objetivo final da aula.
teacher.survival: Survival Card (3 min): 'Five phrases. With these you can sit in front of any committee, anywhere.' Toque cada uma; tom firme, lento, sem desculpa na voz.
teacher.learned: Wrap-up (3 min): Ele clica cada check e diz a frase que prova. So depois devolva as 2 correcoes. Feche o bloco: vinte aulas, vinte conversas que ele evitava. Homework ORAL: gravar 2 minutos de defesa de um problema real.
teacher.closing: Fechamento (1 min): Homework ORAL: gravar 2 minutos de defesa no comite e enviar. Diga que este bloco de 20 acabou: da aula 21 em diante, ele nao sobrevive a sala -- ele lidera a sala.
